proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age (higher age_years) is associated with lower objective_response in this AML cohort.
Female sex (sex_female==1) differs in objective_response from male sex.

analyses.result_summary: Mean age in responders 67.98 vs non-responders 67.99 (Mann-Whitney p=0.70). Response rate is essentially flat across age deciles (range 0.178-0.199). No detectable age effect.
Response rate 0.189 in females vs 0.186 in males (chi-square p=0.42). No sex difference in overall response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ECOG performance status (ecog_ps) is associated with lower objective_response.
Patients with secondary_aml==1 have lower objective_response than patients with de novo AML.
Patients flagged unfit_for_intensive==1 have lower objective_response than fit patients (poor reserve hypothesis).
Patients with complex_karyotype==1 have lower objective_response than patients without.
Patients with tp53_mutation==1 have lower objective_response than TP53 wild-type patients.

analyses.result_summary: Mean ECOG 0.67 in responders vs 0.83 in non-responders; higher ECOG predicts lower response (Mann-Whitney p=7.4e-92). Direction matches hypothesis.
Response 0.188 in secondary_aml vs 0.187 in de novo (chi-square p=0.85). No detectable effect.

Response 0.221 in unfit vs 0.165 in fit (chi-square p=1.9e-55). Direction OPPOSITE to hypothesis (unfit responds MORE often). Likely confounded by treatment assignment (V/A is preferentially given to unfit).
Response 0.168 in complex_karyotype vs 0.191 without (chi-square p=3.3e-6). Direction matches hypothesis.
Response 0.166 in TP53-mutated vs 0.190 in wild-type (chi-square p=3.5e-5). Direction matches hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation==1 have higher objective_response than NPM1 wild-type (favorable AML mutation).
Patients with flt3_itd==1 have lower objective_response than FLT3-ITD-negative patients (adverse risk).
Patients with flt3_tkd==1 have lower objective_response than FLT3-TKD-negative patients.
Patients with idh1_mutation==1 differ in objective_response from IDH1 wild-type.
Patients with idh2_mutation==1 differ in objective_response from IDH2 wild-type.

analyses.result_summary: Response 0.235 in NPM1-mutated vs 0.167 in wild-type (chi-square p=5.8e-70). Direction matches hypothesis; NPM1 strongly favors response.
Response 0.189 in FLT3-ITD+ vs 0.187 in negative (chi-square p=0.64). No detectable main effect (will be examined in interactions).
Response 0.180 in FLT3-TKD+ vs 0.188 in negative (chi-square p=0.34). No detectable main effect.
Response 0.191 in IDH1+ vs 0.187 in wild-type (chi-square p=0.60). No detectable main effect.
Response 0.191 in IDH2+ vs 0.187 in wild-type (chi-square p=0.47). No detectable main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with lower objective_response (hyperleukocytosis adverse).
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with lower objective_response.
Lower albumin_g_dl is associated with lower objective_response (poor nutrition / inflammation).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower objective_response (high tumor burden).

analyses.result_summary: WBC mean 19.27 in responders vs 20.39 in non-responders (Mann-Whitney p=2.6e-8). Higher WBC predicts lower response; matches hypothesis.
Blast% mean 58.5 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders (Mann-Whitney p=4.4e-17). Higher blast burden predicts lower response.
Albumin mean 3.82 in responders vs 3.80 in non-responders (Mann-Whitney p=1.7e-7). Lower albumin predicts lower response; direction matches.
LDH mean 223.1 in responders vs 224.0 in non-responders (Mann-Whitney p=0.25). No detectable LDH effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Larger weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower objective_response (cachexia/poor reserve).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower objective_response (inflammation).
Higher nlr (neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio) is associated with lower objective_response.

Liver/kidney chemistry, electrolytes (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, hemoglobin) have no meaningful association with objective_response after main-effect screening.

analyses.result_summary: Weight loss mean 3.44% in responders vs 3.95% in non-responders (Mann-Whitney p=7.5e-27). Larger loss → lower response; matches.
CRP mean 5.69 in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders (Mann-Whitney p=1.6e-6). Higher CRP → lower response; matches.
NLR mean 3.50 in both groups (Mann-Whitney p=0.69). No NLR effect.

analyses.code: mannwhitneyu for AST, ALT, ALP, total_bili, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, hemoglobin
analyses.result_summary: All p-values > 0.05 except alkaline_phosphatase_u_l (responders 105.2 vs 104.1, p=0.10) and AST (p=0.08). Hb mean 12.48 vs 12.51 (p=0.15). No clinically meaningful main effect for chemistry/electrolytes/Hb.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response vs not receiving midostaurin (overall, unstratified).
treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).
treatment_7plus3 increases objective_response vs not receiving it (overall).

analyses.result_summary: Response 0.186 on midostaurin vs 0.188 off (chi-square p=0.67). No main-effect benefit.
Response 0.181 on gilteritinib vs 0.189 off (chi-square p=0.14). No main-effect benefit; trend slightly negative.
Response 0.188 on ivosidenib vs 0.187 off (chi-square p=0.93). No main effect.
Response 0.186 on enasidenib vs 0.188 off (chi-square p=0.79). No main effect.
Response 0.220 on V/A vs 0.166 off (chi-square p<1e-50). Strong positive main effect, +5.4% absolute. Direction matches.
Response 0.189 on 7+3 vs 0.186 off (chi-square p=0.47). No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_itd==1 patients (canonical FLT3-targeted use).
treatment_midostaurin increases objective_response specifically in flt3_tkd==1 patients.

analyses.code: stratified by flt3_itd; smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_midostaurin * flt3_itd')
analyses.result_summary: Among FLT3-ITD+: response 0.176 on midostaurin vs 0.191 off (diff -1.5%, chi-square p=0.19). Logit interaction term coef -0.106, p=0.19. NO benefit; trend slightly negative. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Among FLT3-TKD+: response 0.176 on midostaurin vs 0.181 off (diff -0.5%, p=0.85). Logit interaction p=0.87. NO benefit. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response in flt3_itd==1 patients (single-agent FLT3 inhibitor for relapsed/refractory FLT3+ AML).
treatment_gilteritinib increases objective_response in flt3_tkd==1 patients.
treatment_gilteritinib benefit is concentrated in FLT3-ITD+ patients who are unfit_for_intensive==1.

analyses.result_summary: Among FLT3-ITD+: response 0.191 on gilteritinib vs 0.189 off (diff +0.2%, p=0.86). Logit interaction p=0.32. NO benefit. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Among FLT3-TKD+: response 0.151 on gilteritinib vs 0.186 off (diff -3.5%, p=0.10). Trend negative; not supported.
Among FLT3-ITD+ unfit: response 0.245 on gilteritinib vs 0.216 off (diff +2.9%, p=0.13). Direction positive but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_ivosidenib increases objective_response in idh1_mutation==1 patients (canonical IDH1-targeted use).
Within IDH1-mutated patients, ivosidenib benefit is concentrated in tp53_mutation==0 / complex_karyotype==0 cytogenetically favorable subset.

analyses.result_summary: Among IDH1+: response 0.154 on ivosidenib vs 0.194 off (diff -4.0%, p=0.11). Direction OPPOSITE to canonical expectation; trend slightly harmful. Logit interaction coef -0.30, p=0.077. Hypothesis NOT supported.
Among IDH1+/TP53wt/no complex karyotype: response 0.160 on ivosidenib vs 0.194 off (diff -3.4%, p=0.26). Still no benefit even after restriction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response in idh2_mutation==1 patients (canonical IDH2-targeted use).
treatment_enasidenib benefit is concentrated in unfit, IDH2-mutated patients.

analyses.result_summary: Among IDH2+: response 0.195 on enasidenib vs 0.191 off (diff +0.4%, p=0.92). No benefit. Logit interaction coef +0.04, p=0.79.
Among IDH2+/unfit: response 0.291 on enasidenib vs 0.231 off (diff +6.0%, p=0.13). Trend positive but not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a substantially larger effect on objective_response in unfit_for_intensive==1 patients than in fit patients (canonical VIALE-A pattern).

analyses.result_summary: Among unfit: response 0.308 on V/A vs 0.163 off (diff +14.5%, p=7e-130). Among fit: 0.160 on V/A vs 0.168 off (diff -0.8%, p=0.08). Logit interaction coef +0.88, p=1.1e-78. STRONGLY supported: V/A only works in unfit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a larger effect on objective_response in npm1_mutation==1 patients than in NPM1 wild-type (NPM1+ is a known V/A super-responder marker).

analyses.result_summary: Among NPM1+: response 0.347 on V/A vs 0.160 off (diff +18.7%, p<1e-150). Logit interaction coef +1.05, p=5.5e-102. Hypothesis STRONGLY supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is attenuated (or abolished) in tp53_mutation==1 patients (TP53 mutation as resistance driver).

analyses.result_summary: Among TP53wt: V/A 0.226 vs 0.166 off (diff +6.0%, p=8e-57). Among TP53+: V/A 0.164 vs 0.167 off (diff -0.4%, p=0.77). Logit interaction coef -0.41, p=4.7e-7. Hypothesis SUPPORTED: TP53 mutation abolishes the V/A benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is attenuated in complex_karyotype==1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Logit interaction coef -0.31, p=6.0e-6. V/A benefit is reduced (but not entirely abolished) in patients with complex karyotype. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 reduces objective_response in complex_karyotype==1 patients (chemo less effective in adverse cytogenetics).
treatment_7plus3 effect on objective_response does NOT differ between unfit_for_intensive and fit patients (negative-control: there should be no fit-only benefit signal).

analyses.result_summary: Among complex_karyotype+: response 0.155 on 7+3 vs 0.179 off (diff -2.3%, p=0.008). Logit interaction coef -0.21, p=0.0015. SUPPORTED: 7+3 is mildly harmful in complex karyotype.

Among fit: response 0.167 on 7+3 vs 0.163 off (diff +0.4%, p=0.38). Among unfit: 0.221 vs 0.221 (diff 0.000, p=0.99). Logit interaction p=0.56. As expected, no differential benefit; 7+3 is essentially null on response in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive==1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response further when also npm1_mutation==1 (joint subgroup, additive enhancement).

analyses.code: subset unfit==1 & npm1==1; chi2 V/A vs not
analyses.result_summary: Among unfit + NPM1+: response 0.633 on V/A (n=2401) vs 0.160 off (n=3585), absolute diff +47.4%, chi2 p<1e-300. Hypothesis STRONGLY supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive==1, npm1_mutation==1 patients, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is further amplified when tp53_mutation==0 (TP53 wild-type required).

analyses.result_summary: Among unfit + NPM1+ + TP53wt: response 0.691 on V/A (n=2125) vs 0.157 off (n=3224); diff +53.4%, p=0. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among unfit_for_intensive==1, npm1_mutation==1, tp53_mutation==0, complex_karyotype==0 patients (full joint subgroup), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces objective_response in roughly 4-5x the rate seen off-treatment.

analyses.code: subset unfit==1 & npm1==1 & tp53==0 & complex==0; chi2
analyses.result_summary: Among the full 4-condition subgroup: response 0.786 on V/A (n=1790) vs 0.158 off (n=2741); absolute diff +62.8%, RR ~5x, p=0. STRONGLY supported and is the maximal V/A subgroup found.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the joint subgroup unfit & NPM1+ & TP53wt & no complex karyotype, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has NO effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: subset NOT(unfit & npm1 & tp53wt & no_complex); chi2
analyses.result_summary: In the complement (n=45,469): response 0.164 on V/A vs 0.167 off (diff -0.3%, chi2 p=0.42). The complement has essentially no V/A treatment effect. Hypothesis SUPPORTED — V/A benefit is fully concentrated in the 4-variable subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each of the four predicates (unfit_for_intensive==1, npm1_mutation==1, tp53_mutation==0, complex_karyotype==0) is individually necessary to obtain the maximal V/A response benefit; dropping any one substantially shrinks the absolute effect.

analyses.result_summary: Full subgroup diff +62.8%. Drop unfit: +24.3% (NPM1+ & TP53wt & no complex only). Drop NPM1: +18.6%. Drop TP53wt requirement (allow TP53+): +55.6%. Drop no-complex requirement: +53.4%. Each predicate (especially unfit and NPM1) materially contributes — unfit and NPM1 are the dominant drivers, with TP53wt and no-complex providing additional uplift. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression adjusting for all baseline features and treatments simultaneously, treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine remains the only treatment with an independent positive effect on objective_response, while the other five treatments are null.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted log-odds: V/A +0.362 (p<1e-50), 7+3 +0.014 (p=0.55), midostaurin -0.009 (p=0.79), gilteritinib -0.050 (p=0.13), ivosidenib +0.019 (p=0.65), enasidenib -0.011 (p=0.80). Confirmatory significant adjusted predictors: ECOG (-0.36), unfit_for_intensive (+0.37), NPM1 (+0.44), complex karyotype (-0.17), TP53 (-0.19), albumin (+0.12), blast% (-0.005), weight loss (-0.04), CRP (-0.007), WBC (-0.002). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When the V/A interactions with unfit_for_intensive, npm1_mutation, tp53_mutation, complex_karyotype are added to the multivariable model, the V/A main effect becomes negative (i.e., V/A is harmful in the off-target subgroup) while the four interactions remain large and significant.

analyses.result_summary: V/A main effect -0.379 (p=2e-21). Interactions: V/A:unfit +0.95 (p<1e-50), V/A:npm1 +1.13 (p<1e-50), V/A:tp53 -0.51 (p=2e-9), V/A:complex -0.35 (p=5e-7). Net log-odds for V/A in unfit+NPM1+TP53wt+nocomplex ≈ -0.38 + 0.95 + 1.13 = +1.70 (about 5.5× odds boost); in fit/NPM1- patients, -0.38 (slight harm). Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Systematic 3-way binary subgroup screening (constrained to n_on>=200) identifies treatment-specific subgroups with treatment effects beyond V/A: 7+3 is harmful in complex_karyotype subgroups (esp. complex+FLT3-ITD+NPM1), enasidenib trends positive in NPM1+ female patients, and the targeted FLT3/IDH inhibitors fail to show benefit even in their canonical mutation-defined subgroups.

analyses.result_summary: Top 7+3 negative subgroups all involve complex_karyotype: complex+FLT3-ITD+NPM1 diff -12.0% (p=0.0011); complex+IDH1+ -7.1% to -7.8% (p=0.03-0.04). Top enasidenib positives: unfit+NPM1+female +8.4% (p=0.012); no-complex+NPM1+female +6.7% (p=0.0017); NPM1+female+secondary_aml=0 +5.9% (p=0.008). Effect persists in IDH2- among NPM1+females, suggesting it is not IDH2-driven but a sex×NPM1 interaction. V/A subgroups dominate, with all top-positive subgroups containing unfit + NPM1+. Hypothesis broadly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In NPM1-mutated female patients, treatment_enasidenib increases objective_response (a sex-modified enasidenib effect not requiring IDH2 mutation).
The 3-way interaction enasidenib × sex_female × npm1_mutation on objective_response is statistically significant.

analyses.result_summary: Among female + NPM1+: response 0.279 on enasidenib (n=542) vs 0.229 off (n=6147), diff +5.0%, chi2 p=0.0099. Among female + NPM1+ + IDH2+: +2.5% (p=0.79; small n=60). Among female + NPM1+ + IDH2-: +5.2% (p=0.011). Among male + NPM1+: -3.5% (p=0.052). Subgroup signal is real but modest and not driven by IDH2.

Three-way interaction term coef +0.239, p=0.18. Two-way enasidenib×sex_female coef +0.226 (p=0.035). The sex modifier of enasidenib is significant; the three-way term is suggestive but not significant. Hypothesis h51 NOT confirmed at conventional alpha; h50 supported descriptively.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response specifically in patients meeting all four conditions: unfit_for_intensive==1 AND npm1_mutation==1 AND tp53_mutation==0 AND complex_karyotype==0. Outside this 4-variable subgroup, V/A produces no detectable change in response. The unfavorable predicates that suppress the V/A treatment effect are tp53_mutation==1 and complex_karyotype==1; the necessary favorable predicates are unfit_for_intensive==1 and npm1_mutation==1. This is the dominant heterogeneous treatment effect in the cohort.

FINAL secondary: For treatment_7plus3, the treatment-effect heterogeneity is concentrated in adverse cytogenetics — 7+3 reduces objective_response in complex_karyotype==1 patients and is essentially neutral elsewhere. No favorable subgroup in which 7+3 increases response was identified.

FINAL secondary: For treatment_midostaurin, treatment_gilteritinib, treatment_ivosidenib, and treatment_enasidenib, no clinically meaningful or statistically significant subgroup of objective_response benefit was identified — including their canonical mutation-targeted subgroups (FLT3-ITD/TKD, IDH1, IDH2). Enasidenib showed a borderline positive signal in NPM1+ females but the three-way interaction was not significant.

analyses.code: joint subgroup (unfit==1 & npm1==1 & tp53==0 & complex==0): on=1790 vs off=2741
analyses.result_summary: Confirmatory: response 0.786 on V/A vs 0.158 off, diff +62.8%, p=0 (chi-square exhausts precision). Outside this 4-predicate subgroup (n=45,469), V/A diff -0.3% (p=0.42). The full subgroup definition is the maximal-effect subgroup detected by both interaction screening and exhaustive 3-binary-feature search.

Among complex_karyotype==1 (n=7462): response 0.155 on 7+3 vs 0.179 off, diff -2.3%, p=0.008. In complex+FLT3-ITD+NPM1: -12.0% (p=0.001). Outside complex_karyotype: 7+3 diff +0.7% (p=0.06). Confirmatory.

analyses.code: subset to canonical biomarker for each targeted therapy and chi2 each
analyses.result_summary: Midostaurin in FLT3-ITD+: -1.5% (p=0.19). Gilteritinib in FLT3-ITD+: +0.2% (p=0.86). Ivosidenib in IDH1+: -4.0% (p=0.11; trend NEGATIVE). Enasidenib in IDH2+: +0.4% (p=0.92). None reach significance; some trend opposite to canonical clinical expectation. Adjusted multivariable coefficients confirm null. Enasidenib in NPM1+ females +5.0% (p=0.010) is the only borderline secondary signal.
